question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set a crafted `X-Real-IP` header containing newline characters (e.g., `\\nFAKE 200 OK`) to inject false log entries into the logrus output, causing operators to misread network partition events and delay incident response, leading to prolonged undetected network isolation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Forwarded-For` to an arbitrarily long string (e.g., 1 MB of repeated characters) on every request to exhaust heap memory in the logging pipeline, causing the Rosetta process to OOM-crash and partition the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the server with high-volume requests to non-`internalPaths` endpoints, forcing every request to emit a `log.Info` entry, exhausting disk I/O and filling the filesystem so that the process hangs on log writes and becomes unreachable, effectively partitioning the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with extremely long `request.URL.RequestURI()` values (e.g., 10 MB query strings) to cause each `fmt.Sprintf` log message to allocate large strings, exhausting heap memory and crashing the process, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user inject ANSI escape sequences into the `X-Real-IP` header (e.g., `\\x1b[2J`) so that when logs are rendered in a terminal-based monitoring dashboard, the display is corrupted, blinding operators to a real network partition event?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that contain newline characters (e.g., `GET /network/list%0aFAKE-ENTRY`) so that `request.URL.RequestURI()` injects a fake log line into the structured log output, masking a genuine network partition alert?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user spoof the `X-Real-IP` header to impersonate a trusted monitoring agent's IP address, causing the real monitoring agent's legitimate network-partition alerts to be attributed to the wrong source and dismissed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Forwarded-For` to a comma-separated list of thousands of IP addresses (e.g., `1.2.3.4, 5.6.7.8, ...` repeated 10,000 times) to cause the entire string to be logged verbatim, bloating log files and exhausting disk space, triggering a process hang that partitions the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of concurrent requests to force `time.Now()` and `time.Since(start)` to be called at high frequency, creating a goroutine pile-up that exhausts the Go runtime scheduler and starves the health-check handler, causing liveness probes to time out and the node to be removed from the network by an orchestrator?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: tracingResponseWriter.Write()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response that calls `Write()` multiple times (chunked response) so that `w.data` is overwritten on each call, causing the last chunk only to be stored, and if a health-check response is truncated this way, the health controller returns a malformed JSON body that causes the orchestrator to mark the node as unhealthy and partition it?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request path that exactly matches `/health/liveness` but with a trailing null byte (`/health/liveness\\x00`) so that `internalPaths[path]` returns false, causing the liveness probe log to be emitted at `log.Info` level instead of `log.Debug`, flooding production log aggregators and triggering rate-limit-based log drops that hide a real partition event?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths like `/health/liveness?injected=true` so that `request.URL.RequestURI()` returns a string not present in `internalPaths`, causing the request to be logged at `log.Info` instead of `log.Debug`, and if done at high volume, saturating the log pipeline and masking a network partition signal?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to an IPv6 address with embedded control characters to cause `net.SplitHostPort` fallback logic to behave unexpectedly in downstream log parsers, corrupting structured log fields and preventing automated partition-detection rules from firing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a sustained stream of requests with unique, long paths to prevent log deduplication from working, causing log storage to fill up and the logging subsystem to block, stalling the HTTP server goroutines and partitioning the node from inbound connections?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user omit both `X-Real-IP` and `X-Forwarded-For` headers and send requests from a source address with a malformed `RemoteAddr` (e.g., no port component) so that `net.SplitHostPort` returns an empty string and an error, causing the logged IP to be empty and breaking IP-based network partition detection rules in the SIEM?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with HTTP methods containing special characters (e.g., `GET\\nFAKE`) so that `request.Method` injects a newline into the `fmt.Sprintf` log message, splitting one log entry into two and injecting a fake entry that mimics a network partition alert?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: tracingResponseWriter.WriteHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response path that calls `WriteHeader()` multiple times (which is a misuse of `http.ResponseWriter`), causing `w.statusCode` to be overwritten with a later value, so that the logged status code does not reflect the actual response, hiding error conditions that indicate a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large number of slow HTTP requests (slowloris-style) that each hold a goroutine inside `inner.ServeHTTP(tracingResponseWriter, request)` indefinitely, exhausting the goroutine pool and preventing health-check requests from being served, causing the orchestrator to declare the node partitioned?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to a value like `127.0.0.1` to make their requests appear to originate from localhost, bypassing IP-based rate limiting or firewall rules that are applied after log parsing, allowing them to flood the node with requests that exhaust resources and cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with `Content-Length` set to a very large value without sending the body, causing `inner.ServeHTTP` to block waiting for the body, holding goroutines open and eventually exhausting the server's connection pool, partitioning the node from legitimate peers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a tab character into `X-Forwarded-For` (e.g., `1.2.3.4\\t5.6.7.8`) so that log parsers that split on whitespace misparse the log entry, causing the client IP field to be misidentified and breaking automated network partition detection that relies on IP correlation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path of exactly `/metrics` followed by URL-encoded characters that decode to the same string but are not in `internalPaths` (e.g., `/%6detrics`), causing metrics-endpoint traffic to be logged at `log.Info` level and flooding the log pipeline, degrading observability during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: tracingResponseWriter.Write()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response handler that writes a very large body (e.g., 100 MB), causing `w.data = data` to hold a large byte slice in memory for the duration of the request, and if many such requests are concurrent, exhausting heap memory and crashing the process, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `time.Now()` race condition by opening thousands of connections simultaneously, causing the `start` timestamp to be captured far before `time.Since(start)` is computed, logging artificially inflated latencies that trigger automated circuit-breaker rules and cause the node to be isolated from the network?
